Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible early renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate renal tubular dysfunction or possible urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further evaluation for renal disease and possible urinary tract infection; clinical correlation is advised.
